covers: opening …/#<root-CID> exposes the verified root on self.
the booted root SELF-VERIFIES against the link's content
a WRONG CID self-verifies as FALSE (the badge is honest,
no fragment → no false claim (cid null, boots normally)

results.name: opening …/#<root-CID> exposes the verified root on self.__holoRoot
the booted root SELF-VERIFIES against the link's content-address (verified ✓)
a WRONG CID self-verifies as FALSE (the badge is honest, not cosmetic)
no fragment → no false claim (cid null, boots normally)

spec: delta A (wired) — opening the OS at the shareable link …/#<root-CID> in a real browser self-verifies the booted boot-root against the link's content-address (self.__holoRoot.verified). Correct CID ⇒ true; wrong CID ⇒ false; no fragment ⇒ no claim. The page-level loader reads the fragment (the SW cannot) and re-derives (Law L5).
